Description: Use mod to declare submodules; Rust requires explicit declaration of every module
Do: Declare modules with mod in the parent; use mod.rs or filename.rs for module files
Dont: Forget to declare a module then wonder why it is not found by the compiler

Description: Control visibility with pub, pub(crate), pub(super), or private (default)
Do: Use pub only for true public API; use pub(crate) for crate-internal; use pub(super) for parent-module access; keep fields private by default
Dont: Make everything pub; expose internal types to crate consumers

Description: Choose between file-based (foo.rs) and directory-based (foo/mod.rs) module layouts
Do: Prefer the new style (foo.rs + foo/ directory) over foo/mod.rs; use directory when a module has submodules
Dont: Mix mod.rs and new-style in the same project; use deeply nested mod.rs files

Keywords: pub use, re-export, facade, API surface
Description: Use pub use to create a clean public API surface by re-exporting items from internal modules
Do: Re-export key types at the crate root or module level; create a prelude module for frequently used items
Dont: Force users to navigate deep module paths; re-export everything indiscriminately

Description: Create a prelude module that re-exports the most commonly used items for glob import convenience
Do: Keep preludes small (< 15 items); only include traits and types used in most files; document what the prelude contains
Dont: Put everything in the prelude; make the prelude required for basic usage

Description: Organize use declarations for readability with nested paths and aliases
Do: Group use declarations: std first, external crates second, crate-local third; use nested paths; use aliases for conflicts
Dont: Use glob imports (use module::*) outside of prelude/test contexts; have overly verbose use blocks

Description: Centralize dependency versions in the workspace Cargo.toml to avoid version mismatches
Do: Define dependencies in [workspace.dependencies]; use dep.workspace = true in member Cargo.toml files
Dont: Specify versions independently in each workspace member leading to version conflicts
Example:
# Root Cargo.toml
[workspace]
members = ["crates/*"]

[workspace.dependencies]
tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full"] }
serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
sqlx = { version = "0.7", features = ["postgres", "runtime-tokio"] }

# crates/api/Cargo.toml
[dependencies]
tokio.workspace = true
serde.workspace = true

Description: Use Cargo features for optional functionality and conditional compilation
Do: Define features in Cargo.toml; use #[cfg(feature = )] for conditional code; use default features wisely
Dont: Enable all features by default; create features that the crate does not work without
Example:
# Cargo.toml
[features]
default = ["json"]
json = ["dep:serde_json"]
xml = ["dep:quick-xml"]
full = ["json", "xml"]

// In code:
#[cfg(feature = "json")]
pub fn to_json(val: &impl Serialize) -> String {
    serde_json::to_string(val).unwrap()
}

Description: Use #[cfg()] attributes and cfg!() macro for platform-specific or condition-specific code
Do: Use #[cfg(test)] for test modules; #[cfg(target_os)] for platform-specific code; cfg!() for runtime branching
Dont: Use runtime if/else for things knowable at compile time; abuse cfg for application logic

Description: Understand when to have both lib.rs and main.rs in the same crate
Do: Put reusable logic in lib.rs; put CLI/entry point in main.rs; use lib crate from main for testability
Dont: Put all logic in main.rs making it untestable as a library; duplicate code between lib and main

Keywords: build.rs, build script, codegen, compile time
Description: Use build.rs for code generation, native library compilation, and build-time configuration
Do: Use build.rs for protobuf/gRPC codegen; for linking C libraries; for embedding git info; for cfg flag generation
Dont: Use build.rs for things that can be done at runtime; make build.rs overly complex

Description: Use path dependencies for local development and workspace members; publish to crates.io for external use
Do: Use path dependencies within workspaces; use version + path for publishable crates; use git dependencies for unpublished external crates
Dont: Use path dependencies for production code from a different repo; hardcode absolute paths
